title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While driving toward Oxford Street, Bunder and Anthony discuss Virginia. Bunder claims to have inside information regarding Virginia’s marriage to the late Tim Ruff, asserting that she never loved him despite his intense passion and threats of suicide. According to Bunder, Virginia only married Tim because of his overwhelming devotion. Bunder describes Virginia as a highly intelligent woman who helped Tim's career in Heslovakia and deeply values her freedom. After Bunder drops Anthony off at the corner of Hyde Park, Anthony takes a taxi to Victoria Station and travels to Dover.

In Dover, Anthony seeks directions to Hirstmill, the last house on Langleys Lane. He finds the property abandoned and secluded, surrounded by overgrown gardens. Approaching the house stealthily, Anthony observes a short, stocky foreign man acting as a guard. He follows the guard to an open area and peers through a window, discovering six men gathered around a table: four robust men with Magyar features and two small, agile men. The group speaks French and engages in a heated argument regarding their "boss," whom they are waiting for. One man identifies the leader as Victor, noting that Victor does not tolerate defiance. The men also mention a prisoner whose head wound has healed. Observing the group's lack of discipline and the tension between the different factions, Anthony decides he wants to give "King Victor's" headquarters a lesson.
